name: Shark Hors D'oeuvres
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine 1/4 cup lemon juice (or lime juice), 1/4 cup dry white wine, and 2 cloves of crushed garlic in a bowl. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
2. Add 2 pounds of shark meat cut into 2-inch chunks to the marinade. Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour, or longer if desired.
3. In a separate bowl, mix together seasoned all-purpose flour, beaten eggs, and bread crumbs.
4. Dredge each fish chunk in the seasoned flour mixture, coating it evenly with salt and pepper before dipping into the beaten eggs and then the bread crumbs.
5. Heat vegetable oil in a deep skillet or pot to about 350°F (175°C).
6. Carefully lower the coated fish chunks into the hot oil using tongs or a slotted spoon, frying until golden brown.
7. Using a slotted spoon, remove the fried fish from the oil and drain on paper towels to absorb excess grease.
8. Serve the shark hors d'oeuvres with Creole mustard or tartar sauce.
